name: Pan Burritos
recipeInstructions:
Our family loves Mexican food, so this flavorful, satisfying casserole is a favorite. It's nice to have a way to get the taste of burritos and be able to cut servings any size you want.
1. In a saucepan, combine the Enchilada sauce mix, water, tomato paste, minced garlic, pepper, and salt. Simmer for 15-20 minutes.
2. In a skillet, brown the ground beef and drain. Stir in one-third of the simmered sauce.
3. Spread another third of the sauce on the bottom of a greased 13x9x2 inches baking pan.
4. Place three tortillas over the sauce, tearing to fit the bottom of the pan. Spoon half of the meat mixture over the tortillas and sprinkle with 1 1/2 cups shredded cheese.
5. Add three more tortillas. Spread refried beans over the tortillas, then top with the remaining meat mixture.
6. Sprinkle with another 1 1/2 cups shredded cheese.
7. Layer the remaining tortillas and sauce on top, then sprinkle with the remaining cheese.
8. Bake, uncovered, at 350 degrees Fahrenheit for 35-40 minutes. Let stand 10 minutes before cutting.
9. Serve with taco sauce, sour cream, chopped onion, and/or guacamole if desired.
